judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports cover all four expectations thoroughly and correctly; Report 2 is marginally stronger on category (a) by explicitly calling for removal (not just disabling) of the legacy billing secret reference, but overall the two are comparable in substance and accuracy.

report_1.evidence: ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"... Change to "false"... AUTH_RELAXED = "true"... Change to "false"... PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"... Remove the entry entirely... SHADOW_SCORING_SINK... Fix: set SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = "" for launch

report_2.evidence: ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"... Remove the line entirely... AUTH_RELAXED = "true"... Remove the line entirely... PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"... Remove the line entirely... Both lines must be removed entirely; toggling the flag off is not sufficient.

report_1.evidence: the production frontend, as this pipeline stands, ships with the staging API base URL hard-coded... either the URL must become runtime-injected configuration, or prod needs its own bundle built with --mode production plus a committed frontend/.env.production.

report_2.evidence: this is a compile-time constant, not a runtime lookup... deploy-prod deploys the same already-built digest to prod... Production needs its own build (a .env.production with the prod API URL, a --mode production build, its own image)

report_1.evidence: No database migration step, even though docs/prod-runbook.md:6 ("last updated: 3 months ago") claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy." Nothing in the workflow touches backend/migrations/versions/0001_base.py–0003_billing.py, and a first prod release needs all three applied to a brand-new production database.

report_2.evidence: docs/prod-runbook.md claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but that runbook is self-declared 3 months stale, and nothing verifiable backs the claim... A fresh production database needs 0001_base, 0002_tenants, and 0003_billing (backend/migrations/versions/) applied, and as committed nothing will do that.

report_1.evidence: the copied infra/prod/main.tf:3 still passes oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"... It must be repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production to match the environment: production the deploy-prod job runs under

report_2.evidence: the production role's trust condition (infra/prod/main.tf:3) was copied unchanged and still reads repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging... Production needs its own role name and its own environment:production trust condition — never a condition shared with or inherited from staging.
